refined_fr.refined_fr: CR-NGHE-006.1 (Worksite Feature Mapping): The NGHE solution shall accurately identify, classify, and map key worksite features including terrain contours, static obstacles (e.g., rocks, structures), dynamic obstacles (e.g., other vehicles, personnel), and defined work zones (e.g., excavation areas, haul roads) to support operational planning and real-time execution.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR 'Worksite mapping/modeling' is too broad. This refinement breaks it down into the core functional elements of identification, classification, and mapping of specific feature types, which are essential for subsequent planning and execution functions. It also clarifies the purpose of this mapping.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Generate a test worksite with known terrain, static obstacles, dynamic obstacles, and work zones. Deploy the NGHE solution and compare its generated map/model against the ground truth data for accuracy, completeness, and classification correctness. Verify update rates for dynamic elements.
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-006.1.1 (Terrain Contour Identification): The NGHE solution shall identify and map terrain contours with specified accuracy.
CR-NGHE-006.1.2 (Static Obstacle Detection & Classification): The NGHE solution shall detect, classify, and map static obstacles.
CR-NGHE-006.1.3 (Dynamic Obstacle Detection & Tracking): The NGHE solution shall detect, classify, track, and map dynamic obstacles.
CR-NGHE-006.1.4 (Work Zone Definition & Mapping): The NGHE solution shall allow definition and mapping of work zones.

candidate_dps: Sensor Fusion & SLAM (Simultaneous Localization and Mapping) for environmental perception and map generation.
Pre-computed GIS (Geographic Information System) data combined with manual input for static features.
Distributed sensor network with individual processing nodes feeding a central map server.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-NGHE-006.1 (Sensor Fusion & SLAM): Implement a sensor fusion pipeline integrating data from multiple onboard sensors (e.g., LiDAR, cameras, GNSS, IMU) with a SLAM algorithm to generate and continuously update a 3D semantic map of the worksite, identifying terrain, static obstacles, dynamic obstacles, and work zones.

potential_sources: Tight coupling with sensor hardware interfaces.
Dependency on accurate GNSS/IMU for localization.
Interface with planning/execution functions consuming the map data.
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP inherently involves coupling with sensor data streams and downstream consumers of the map. This is expected and manageable through well-defined interfaces. The SLAM approach minimizes external dependencies for map generation itself, beyond raw sensor data.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Sufficient sensor data quality and density are available from onboard sensors.
Assumption: Computational resources on the vehicle are adequate for real-time SLAM processing.
Risk: SLAM accuracy degradation in feature-poor environments or due to sensor failures.
Risk: Semantic classification errors leading to incorrect obstacle identification, especially for safety-critical dynamic obstacles.
Risk: Data latency in map updates for dynamic elements impacting real-time decision making.

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: The Vehicle module is responsible for all onboard sensing, perception, localization, and local environmental modeling functions.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Output of generated worksite map data (terrain, obstacles, work zones) and localization information to external systems (e.g., Teleoperation, Safety Services).

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Input for defining or updating work zone boundaries and operational constraints from external planning systems or operators.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Output of immediate safety-critical obstacle detections (e.g., personnel, other vehicles) to Safety Services.

module_containment_scores.notes: Too high-level. This is a core capability, not the entire solution. Diffusion would be very high as it would touch almost every part of the solution.
Excellent fit. Onboard sensors, real-time processing, and local environmental understanding are primary responsibilities of the Vehicle. This centralizes the perception pipeline.
Teleoperation consumes map data but does not generate it. Assigning generation here would diffuse perception logic across multiple modules and create unnecessary dependencies.
Safety Services consumes map data and obstacle information for safety functions, but it should not be responsible for generating the base map. This would lead to diffusion of core perception logic.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The 'Vehicle' module is the most appropriate owner for the Sensor Fusion & SLAM DP because it directly interfaces with the physical sensors, requires real-time processing capabilities typically resident on the vehicle, and is the primary source of local environmental awareness. Centralizing this within the Vehicle minimizes diffusion of perception logic.
containment_risks: Risk: Overloading the Vehicle module with too many computational responsibilities, impacting performance or resource availability for other critical vehicle functions.
Risk: If map generation requires significant off-board processing (e.g., cloud-based), this assignment might need revision.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP (Sensor Fusion & SLAM) directly addresses the refined FR (Worksite Feature Mapping) by providing a robust technical solution for identifying, classifying, and mapping the specified features. It aligns with the need for real-time, accurate environmental understanding.

diffusion_risks: Potential for other modules (e.g., Teleoperation, Safety Services) to attempt to re-implement or duplicate parts of the perception logic if interfaces are not clear or if they require highly specialized views of the map data.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The DP is well-contained within the 'Vehicle' module. This module is the logical place for sensor integration and real-time environmental perception. The interfaces defined (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert) allow other modules to consume the generated map data without needing to understand the internal workings of the SLAM system.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of the refined FR against the 'Vehicle' module is feasible. The Vehicle module can be tested in controlled environments with known ground truth to assess the accuracy and completeness of its generated worksite map data, as well as the timeliness of updates for dynamic elements.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation is clear: the 'Vehicle' module is responsible for generating the worksite map data, which directly fulfills the refined FR. Other modules consume this data via well-defined interfaces.

decision.justification: The FR has been refined and decomposed into sub-FRs, the DP selection is sound, and the IM assignment is appropriate and well-justified. The gates have passed. The next logical step is to decompose the identified sub-FRs to the next level of detail.
next_level_sub_frs: CR-NGHE-006.1.1 (Terrain Contour Identification): The NGHE solution shall identify and map terrain contours with specified accuracy.
CR-NGHE-006.1.2 (Static Obstacle Detection & Classification): The NGHE solution shall detect, classify, and map static obstacles.
CR-NGHE-006.1.3 (Dynamic Obstacle Detection & Tracking): The NGHE solution shall detect, classify, track, and map dynamic obstacles.
CR-NGHE-006.1.4 (Work Zone Definition & Mapping): The NGHE solution shall allow definition and mapping of work zones.

assumptions: Sufficient sensor data quality and density are available from onboard sensors.
Computational resources on the vehicle are adequate for real-time SLAM processing.
open_risks: SLAM accuracy degradation in feature-poor environments or due to sensor failures.
Semantic classification errors leading to incorrect obstacle identification, especially for safety-critical dynamic obstacles.
Data latency in map updates for dynamic elements impacting real-time decision making.
Potential for other modules to attempt to re-implement or duplicate parts of the perception logic if interfaces are not clear or if they require highly specialized views of the map data.
Overloading the Vehicle module with too many computational responsibilities, impacting performance or resource availability for other critical vehicle functions.
evidence_needed: Performance benchmarks for SLAM algorithms on target hardware.
Interface control documents for map data consumption.
